title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot investigates the scene of Mrs. McKinty’s murder by visiting her former residence. He meets Mrs. Kiddell, whose family moved into the house four months ago following a housing shortage. Mrs. Kiddell clarifies that while the robbery occurred in the upstairs bedroom—which is now filled with the Kiddells' belongings because Mrs. McKinty's niece from Carlow took the previous contents—the victim was actually killed in the living room.

Poirot then interviews Mrs. Elliot, a tall, dark-haired woman who was the first to discover the body. She explains that a baker named Rakin alerted her to the situation. Mrs. Elliot describes finding the bedroom in disarray and discovering Mrs. McKinty in the sitting room with a cracked skull. She notes that money had been stolen and that the murder weapon remains unknown. Furthermore, Mrs. Elliot claims she saw James Bentley walking down the road around eleven o'clock on the day of the murder. She expresses a lack of surprise regarding Bentley's arrest, characterizing him as "crazy" and suggesting that the location where he hid the money was so obvious it seemed as though he intended to be caught.

After speaking with other neighbors who describe Mrs. McKinty as a respected woman with no known enemies, Poirot reflects on the contradictions of the crime. He notes that while the money was left in a way that implicated Bentley, the murder weapon was hidden. Poirot concludes his investigation of the neighbors and enters a post office, where he meets a middle-aged woman named Sweetman.
